glow.note: FULLY DECODED + MATCHED 2026-07-12. MASK (from -[PAEGlow canThrowRenderOutput] @0xceb54): HgcGlow alpha is a SOFT linear ramp a=clamp((luma709-Threshold)/Softness + 0.5, 0, 1), luma = colorMatrixFromDesiredRGBToYCbCr Y-row (Rec.709), bias in hg_Params[0].w = -(Threshold-0.5*Softness)/Softness, luma scale = 1/Softness (FLT_MAX if Softness==0 => hard step); rgb premultiplied by a. COMBINE (HgcGlowCombineFx, verbatim): glowA=clamp(glow.a*gain,0,1) with gain=Opacity(hg_Params[0].x); glowRGB=max(min(glow.rgb*gain, ceiling),0), ceiling = 1.0 or FLT_MAX per the 'allow>1' bool (both cap at 255 in 8-bit); out.rgb=(1-glowA)*orig+glowRGB. glow.ts reads Softness (id4, default 0.2) and applies the exact mask+combine (replaced the old hard-cutoff + screen/additive approximation). Verified vs headless: radius30/thr0.5/soft0.5 17.96->41.66, radius60/thr0.3 15.96->36.92. GUI-GT gate GREEN (Lights__Bloom, the only PAEGlow user, unchanged).

bloom.note: CEILING: HgcBloomThreshold is an affine scale+bias knockout using MAX-channel luma; the Brightness param (>1) feeds the SAME >1 gain that hits the documented working-space brighten curve (see FILTER_RE_PHASE2 consolidated finding). Both Bloom users ramp Threshold 100->3 and stay gate-green; the isolated sweep with Brightness=2 exposes the shared brighten-curve gap.

levels.note: Gamma nested Histogram(1)>RGB(2)>Gamma(5). GUI-GT-verified the net map is pow(x, 1/gamma). Gamma=1 identity.

colorize.note: DECODED 2026-07-12. HgcColorize: luma=dot(rgb sRGB codes, hg_Params[4]=Rec.709 Y-row of colorMatrixFromDesiredRGBToYCbCr); r2=mix(black,white,luma); out=mix(orig,r2, Intensity*Mix). WORKING-SPACE nuance: FCP sRGB->linear DECODES the Remap Black/White endpoints before the lerp (FCP renders in kCGColorSpaceLinearSRGB) and emits the result to the linear framebuffer as-is; the ISOLATED gradient transfer is out=255*(s2l(black)+luma*(s2l(white)-s2l(black))) which matches headless to sepia mad 3.88. BUT shipping the endpoint-linearisation PER-FILTER regressed the GUI-GT gate on the stacked Colorize transitions (Curtains -0.42, Stylized__Slide -0.76, Up-Over -0.52) because FCP keeps the whole chain in linear + encodes once at readback. Per ROADMAP rule 1 (GUI GT = one truth) the shipped colorizeRemapFilter keeps RAW sRGB endpoints; the isolated [gap] at Intensity=1 (sepia ~22 dB) is the per-filter-vs-linear-chain limit, shared with Brightness>1. Intensity=0 exact identity.

fill.note: Fill With(id1) 0=Color; Color(id2 nested RGB); Mix(10001) lerps input->fill. Verified prior: Mix=1 psnr 55, Mix=0.5 psnr 45.

hsv.note: NEUTRALS (verified vs headless): Hue=0, Saturation=0 (0-centered, -1=grayscale), Value=1 (multiplier, applied squared). Identity=(0,0,1). Saturation/Value/grayscale MATCH (39-48 dB). HUE param DECODED 2026-07-12 (-[PAEHSVAdjust canThrowRenderOutput] @0x372f4-0x37350): it is in DEGREES, not turns — FCP wraps to [0,360] then sets hg_Params[0].x=Hue/360+1.0 (the +1 is a no-op inside the shader frac). Fixed hue-saturation.ts to convert deg->turns (was treating the param as turns, 360x off); the 0.25deg sweep case improved 14.97->29.01 dB. FULL hue-rotation remains a [gap] and is NOT probe-verifiable: at Hue=90/180 the headless probe returns the INPUT unchanged (hvi~1.1, IDENTITY guard fires) — the injected filter does not engage FCP’s hue path there (likely the versionAtCreation/dynamicRangeType w25 branch selects the HGColorMatrix sincos path which our factoryID-7 skeleton doesn’t trigger). All 4 shipping HSV users set Hue=0 so hue is gate-neutral regardless. The 29 dB residual on the tiny 0.25deg rotation is the linear-space hexagon reconstruction, shared with the color-pipeline working-space finding.

tint.note: CEILING [P2-TINT1], now better characterised (2026-07-12): the shipping filter is TintFx (UUID 717D6E01), NOT PAETint. TintFx is NOT in the Filters.bundle Mach-O (no HgcTint symbol under it) — it is an FxPlug factory wrapped by a Motion template (Effects/Color/Tint.moef) with a multi-layer param graph (Color id1-3, Color Space id11=3, Amount id100, rig-linked Intensity/Mix). The real transitions DO tint at full intensity (e.g. Stylized__Diagonal Color=(0.30,0.77,0.29), Intensity=1, Color Space=3) but stay gate-green because tint is a minor overlay on wipe/diagonal geometry. The isolated probe here injects HgcTint-style params (Color:1 + Intensity:2) under factoryID 7, which does NOT reproduce TintFx (observed a ~2x red-channel gain + G/B divergence) — so the low sweep PSNR is partly a PROBE param-mapping artifact, not purely an algorithm gap. Correctly matching TintFx needs decoding the .moef param graph + the Color Space=3 leg + the factoryID 21/13 interaction; deferred (gate-green, no isolated reference we trust). The working color space is linear sRGB (RESOLVED, shared with the color-pipeline family).

CEILING [P2-TINT1], now better characterised (2026-07-12): the shipping filter is TintFx (UUID 717D6E01), NOT PAETint. TintFx is NOT in the Filters.bundle Mach-O (no HgcTint symbol under it) — it is an FxPlug factory wrapped by a Motion template (Effects/Color/Tint.moef) with a multi-layer param graph (Color id1-3, Color Space id11=3, Amount id100, rig-linked Intensity/Mix). The real transitions DO tint at full intensity (e.g. Stylized__Diagonal Color=(0.30,0.77,0.29), Intensity=1, Color Space=3) but stay gate-green because tint is a minor overlay on wipe/diagonal geometry. The isolated probe here injects HgcTint-style params (Color:1 + Intensity:2) under factoryID 7, which does NOT reproduce TintFx (observed a ~2x red-channel gain + G/B divergence) — so the low sweep PSNR is partly a PROBE param-mapping artifact, not purely an algorithm gap. Correctly matching TintFx needs decoding the .moef param graph + the Color Space=3 leg + the factoryID 21/13 interaction; deferred (gate-green, no isolated reference we trust). The working color space is linear sRGB (RESOLVED, shared with the color-pipeline family).

bevel.note: PARTIAL MATCH 2026-07-12. PAEBevel is an OFFSET-ACCUMULATION bevel (bevelHe @0x94fec + HgcBevel): it re-draws the layer as 4 directionally-offset light-tinted copies, offset radius = BevelWidth*0.28125*maxDim (band_px; DECODED+HEADLESS-VERIFIED EXACTLY: 0.05->27, 0.1->54, 0.2->108 px @1920). theta=angle*0.5, four |cos(theta+k)| lobes k in {0,-45,-90,+45}. RGB is lit in a flat-tinted band inward from each alpha boundary (FCP treats the FRAME BORDER as a boundary — a full-opaque layer still bevels its frame). ROOT CAUSE of the old 1-px no-op: TS read the NORMALIZED Bevel Width (~0.05) as a raw pixel step (Math.round=0->1px); rewrote bevelFilter to the decoded band-width law + 4-lobe lighting + OOB-as-transparent boundary (TS band now matches headless: 104 vs 105 px @w0.2). CEILING on the isolated PSNR (14-19 dB): the Light Angle param is NOT probe-drivable (like the Luma Keyer key blob / HSV hue / Tint) — injecting a flat Light Angle leaves headless IDENTICAL across 0/45/90/135, so the ANGLE-DEPENDENT lobe rotation + the exact HgcBevel mix(dest,color,vtxAlpha) composite cannot be verified against headless; only the band GEOMETRY (verified exact) and the default top-lit result are observable. Only user Stylized__Panels_Across is gate-neutral (10.36->10.33). Band-width geometry is the headless-verified deliverable; angle lighting is decode-faithful but unverifiable.

PARTIAL 2026-07-12: the BAND-WIDTH LAW is DECODED + headless-verified EXACTLY; the ANGLE-dependent lighting is decoded from disasm but NOT probe-verifiable. bevelHe (Filters, @0x94fec): width*=0.5 in canThrow, theta=angle*0.5, four facet light lobes |cos(theta+k)| for k in {0,-45,-90,+45}, offset-accumulation of 4 tinted layer copies composited by HgcBevel (mix(dest,color,vtxAlpha), union alpha). MEASURED band width from a headless flat-gray/photo probe (Bevel Width 0.05/0.1/0.2 -> 27/54/108 px @maxDim=1920): band_px = BevelWidth*0.28125*maxDim (0.28125=9/32) — Bevel Width is a NORMALIZED fraction, NOT pixels. FCP treats the FRAME BORDER as an alpha boundary (out-of-frame=transparent). Rewrote bevel.ts from the old per-pixel alpha-gradient-normal (which used the raw ~0.05 width as a 1-PX step -> effectively a no-op) to the offset-accumulation band model; TS band width now matches headless to 1px (104 vs 105 @w0.2). CEILING: the Light Angle param (parmId 1) is NOT probe-drivable — headless renders IDENTICALLY for injected angle 0/45/90/135 (its default light is top-bright/bottom-dark/sides-neutral), so the four-lobe ROTATION cannot be verified (same class as the Luma Keyer key blob, HSV hue, Tint). The real user (Stylized__Panels_Across, angle=-4.1rad) applies it to sliding alpha panels; gate-neutral (10.36->10.33). Isolated PSNR is angle-limited (~14-19) because the probe's fixed default light != the injected angle. Light Color(id4) now plumbed.

lumakeyer.note: MATCHED 2026-07-12 (was a false 'ceiling'). HgcLumaKeyer is the LUT keyer: it clamps luma Y (Rec.709), samples a 256-wide LUT built CPU-side in -[PAELumaKeyer createLutForNode:] as lut[i]=OMKeyer2D::getAlphaLuma(i/255), then scale+biases. getAlphaLuma (ProAppsFxSupport @0x3bf94, decoded) is a 4-control-point TRAPEZOID band-pass over luma (A'=(lumA+1)/2..D'=(lumD+1)/2): 0 below A', smoothstep rise A'->B', plateau 1 B'->C', linear fall C'->D', 0 above D'. RGB passes through UNCHANGED (un-premultiplied); only ALPHA is keyed. ROOT CAUSE of the earlier 'not probe-verifiable' verdict: filter_verify compared RGB-ONLY, so an alpha-only keyer looked like near-identity (hvi 1.35) and tripped the identity guard — FIXED filter_verify to compare RGBA (the keyer's matte is now scored). Both shipping users (360/Circle_Wipe, 360/Reveal_Wipe) have a STATIC (numberOfKeypoints=0) Luma keyer blob = the DEFAULT curve, which the ramp probe MEASURED: alpha rises across luma~[0.004,0.067], plateau to ~0.56, linear fall to 0 at 1.0. TS lumaKeyerFilter rewritten to reproduce it: isolated headless PSNR 7.54->34.15 (mae 65.85->1.51), ramp-curve mae 0.019. Gate-neutral (both users are 360-reorient dominated, which renders black headless — separate ceiling): Circle_Wipe 7.47->7.46, Reveal_Wipe 7.45->7.43, 0 regressions.

brightness.note: PAEBrightness = HGColorMatrix diagonal (per-channel MULTIPLY). DECODED 2026-07-12 the full transfer + the ROOT CAUSE of the old 'brighten diverges' gap: FCP's working color space is kCGColorSpaceLinearSRGB (16-bit half-float ExtendedLinearSRGB readback — confirmed by instrumenting OZRenderParams_getWorkingColorSpace in oz_render.mm, OZ_WS_DEBUG). The 8-bit source node is UNTAGGED, so FCP treats code AS linear working values. ISOLATED transfer (synthetic 0..255 gradient, per code v): amount<=1 out=clip(amount*v) (mad 0.24); amount>1 out=srgbEncode(amount*v/255) (mad 0.12) — a hard discontinuity at amount==1 (b=0.95 code-mult, b=1.01 linear-encode). CORRECTION to prior docs: there is exactly ONE shipping PAEBrightness user — Objects__Curtains, amount=2.91 (it BRIGHTENS, not 'all 27 darken'). Applying the isolated brighten-encode PER-FILTER regressed Curtains' GUI-GT gate (14.31->13.85), because Curtains STACKS Brightness->Mono(ChannelMixer) and FCP keeps the WHOLE chain in linear + encodes ONCE at readback (headless Curtains mean 11 vs GUI 30 — the sRGB code-multiply chain lands closest to the GUI truth). So shipped code keeps the plain code-multiply (correct for the darken leg and closest for the stacked brighten user); the isolated brighten leg needs a linear FILTER-CHAIN (encode once after all filters), tracked as an engine-architecture item, NOT a per-filter encode.

channelmixer.note: VERBATIM HgcChannelMixer: out.rgb = per-channel dot(unpremult_rgba, hg_Params[row]) (row 4th col = offset via r1.w=1), re-premultiply, mix(orig, out, Mix). Nested param groups: Red Output(id1)>Red-Red(2)/Red-Green(3)/Red-Blue(4)/Red-Alpha(5); Green Output(6)>7/8/9/10; Blue Output(11)>12/13/14/15; Monochrome(21) sets all 3 rows to the RED-row weights (constrainMonoParams); Mix(10001). Verified vs headless: R<->B swap 42.25, Monochrome(0.3/0.59/0.11) 41.95, R<->B Mix0.5 42.38 — matched. 7 shipping users.

HgcChannelMixer VERBATIM (channel-mixer.ts): un-premultiply -> per-out-channel 4-wide dot (RR/RG/RB weights + '-Alpha' child as the constant OFFSET via r1.w=1) -> clamp alpha -> re-premultiply -> mix(orig,mixed,Mix). Monochrome (id21) = constrainMonoParams: all 3 out rows take the RED-row weights (verified: the old luma601-then-matrix double-processing was wrong). Real param IDs from Movements/Color_Planes.motr: Red Output(1){Red-Red 2, Red-Green 3, Red-Blue 4, Red-Alpha 5}, Green Output(6){7,8,9,10}, Blue Output(11){12,13,14,15}, Monochrome(21), Mix(10001). Verified vs headless: R<->B swap PSNR 42.25, Monochrome(.3/.59/.11) 41.95, R<->B Mix0.5 42.38.

gaussian.note: sigma=radius/6.67 (decoded effective ratio; HGLinearFilter::gaussian is the normalized PDF). PSNR drops with Amount (46@10 -> 36@80) purely from the 1854->1920 conform + decimation resampling on large blurs; mae stays 0.8-2.4 = excellent.

zoom.note: LOG-POLAR (2026-07-12): a concentric-ring probe through headless FCP proved the zoom-blur width GROWS PROPORTIONALLY with radius (rings survive at center, obliterated at edges; higher Amount pushes destruction inward) — displacement is not a fixed pixel count. This is the log-polar polarToRect leg + 1-D Gaussian along the log-radius axis (see directional-blur.ts zoomBlurPolar). Rewritten from the old uniform (linear-radius) model; verified vs headless on rings.png: PSNR 24-29 dB across Amount=5..40. On start.png (smooth photo, the sweep input) PSNR is lower (~18-20 dB) because the polar remap resampling + 1854->1920 conform dominate a smooth image; that is the achievable structural ceiling for this remap filter, not a shape error. sigma_u=Amount*ZOOM_LOG_K(=1.0) log-buffer px. Both shipping users (Blurs/Zoom keyframes Amount from 0, 360/Circle_Wipe) stay gate-green.

directional.note: PAEDirectionalBlur = 1-D Gaussian along the Angle direction, streak = Amount px. Verified vs headless: Amount=50/Angle=0 -> PSNR ~46, Amount=100/Angle=45 -> PSNR ~30 (drops with streak length purely from the 1854->1920 conform). No fix needed; entries pin the match.

zoom.note: LOG-POLAR (2026-07-12): PAEZoomBlur's polarToRect is a LOG-polar remap, so a 1-D Gaussian along the log-radius axis makes the streak length grow PROPORTIONALLY with radius (rings survive near center, obliterated at edges) — proven by a concentric-ring probe (rings.png Amount=5/10/20/40, log-polar PSNR 24-29 dB vs ~16 dB for the old linear-radius uniform model). sigma_u = Amount*ZOOM_LOG_K in ln(r) units, ZOOM_LOG_K=1.0 at the rings-probe optimum plateau. On the smooth start.png probe input the structural match reads ~18-20 dB (Amount 20/50) / ~19 dB (Amount 100), bounded by the 1854->1920 conform + the smooth-image PSNR floor; the ring probe is the honest geometric verifier. Both shipping users (Blurs/Zoom keyframes Amount from 0; 360°/Circle_Wipe wipe-dominated) stay gate-green.

directional.note: PAEDirectionalBlur: 1-D Gaussian along the (cos,sin) Angle axis, sigma=Amount*.../6. Verified vs headless: Amount=50/Angle=0 -> PSNR 46, Amount=100/Angle=45 -> PSNR 30. High PSNR because the blur is axis-aligned and shape-exact; the residual is the 1854->1920 conform on the largest kernels.

scrape.note: axis = (-sin(rot), cos(rot)) — the sincos wiring bakes a +pi/2. rot=0 is the weakest (25.84) because that orientation maximises the 1854->1920 conform artifact; rot=pi/2 hits 39.57. Movements/Smear (rot=3pi/2, the only user) baseline is intentionally re-frozen to the FCP-correct axis render (it scores 0.35 lower on that noisy 11-dB slug).

underwater.note: NOT REGISTERED (apply() is a gate-safe passthrough — see underwater.ts): the FreqSynth field uses an unrecoverable runtime GPU noise texture and the plugin renders black headless for t>1.0, so a faithful impl regresses the gate. filter_verify will report NO FILTER for the passthrough registration; this ceiling entry documents the case. Exercise the faithful underwaterApply() manually if the noise field is ever recovered.
